items.title: Pro-Iran Iraqi militia agrees to five-day pause on US embassy attacks
items.summary: BAGHDAD: An influential pro-Iranian armed group in Iraq pledged early Thursday to stop attacking the US embassy for five days, presenting a series of conditions. Kataeb Hizbollah said the group's secretary-general had "issued orders to suspend operations targeting the US Embassy in Baghdad for a period of five days", but stipulated several conditions, notably preventing Israeli bombing of a southern Beirut neighbourhood, and "a commitment to refrain from bombing residential areas in Baghdad and other provinces." AFP reported no drone or rocket attacks so far on the US embassy in Iraq's capital Baghdad between Wednesday night and Thursday morning.--AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Friday or Saturday? Malaysians ready either way for Aidilfitri
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 19 — Malaysians are heading into Hari Raya Aidilfitri this year better prepared, with many already bracing for the possibility that the celebration could fall either on Friday or Saturday. That year, Aidilfitri had been calculated to fall on May 3. “This year, my family and I expect it to fall on Saturday or Friday.

items.title: Enjoy your iftar dates now: Local wholesalers say prices, supplies unaffected by Middle East conflict but that could change
items.summary: At Al Badr Trading, a date wholesaler in Danau Kota, Setapak, boxes of imported dates — from Saudi Arabia’s prized Mabroom to Iran’s Mariami — are seen stacked neatly, ready for bulk sale during Ramadan. When kept in cold storage, dates can remain in good condition well past their expiry dates, with a shelf life extending up to one to three years. Laili gesturing at a variety of imported dates from the Middle East being sold at Al Badr Trading in Danau Kota, Setapak.

items.title: Iran vows Gulf energy retaliation after US-Israeli strike kills top officials
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran targeted oil and gas facilities across the Gulf on Wednesday after Israel killed the Islamic republic's intelligence chief, marking another blow to its leadership. The death of Esmail Khatib followed that of security chief Ali Larijani, which Iran's new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei -- unseen since succeeding his slain father -- vowed to avenge. Iran's warning came after Qatar's state energy company said an Iranian missile strike sparked a fire causing "extensive damage" at its main gas facility.

items.title: Iranian strikes hit Qatar's Ras Laffan gas hub, causing major damage
items.summary: DOHA: Iranian strikes caused "extensive damage" at the world's largest gas hub in Qatar, the Gulf state's energy firm said Thursday, and an AFP journalist saw a vast fire illuminating the night sky, visible from roughly 30 kilometres away. Earlier, Teheran had vowed to target energy infrastructure across the Gulf following a US-Israeli attack on its own facilities. Iran later warned it would destroy Gulf energy infrastructure if further strikes hit its own energy sector, state media reported.

items.title: MDA cracks down on illegal contact lens trade
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Medical Device Authority (MDA) will widen its enforcement net to target suppliers and wholesalers knowingly distributing unregistered contact lenses to retailers. The operation stemmed from a tip-off by the New Straits Times on the sale of contact lenses without prescriptions, including unregistered products. Retailers of unregistered lenses were instructed to cease operations immediately.

items.title: Gaming anonymity fuels extremist grooming of youth, experts warn
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Online gaming platforms have turned into hunting grounds for extremist recruiters targeting children, experts warned, a discovery made after the detention of three minors who were reportedly radicalised online. International Islamic University Malaysia communications lecturer Dr Shafizan Mohamed said gaming platforms were no longer spaces for only entertainment. The minors were arrested under the Security Offences (Special Measures) Act (Sosma) after allegedly being exposed to Islamic State (IS) doctrine through social media and online games.

items.title: Shrapnel from Iranian missiles kills three Palestinian women in West Bank
items.summary: RAMALLAH: Palestinian Territories: Falling shrapnel that struck a hair salon in the occupied West Bank killed at least three women on Wednesday, marking the first Palestinian deaths of the ongoing Middle East War. The deaths came shortly after Israel's military reported another round of Iranian missile launches. Wafa said missile fragments landed in multiple West Bank locations, including within the city of Hebron.

items.title: All 2,000 chickens sold within 30 minutes
items.summary: All 2,000 chickens, priced at RM7 per kilogramme, were sold within 30 minutes during the Jualan Termurah Madani programme at the Bukit Gantang member of parliament's service centre yesterday. Eager shoppers also bought beef priced as low as RM24 per kilogramme, as well as cooking oil, eggs, flour, sugar and other essential items. Bukit Gantang MP Datuk Syed Abu Hussin Hafiz Syed Abdul Fasal said the bulk-purchase approach was introduced to provide cheaper goods without relying on government subsidies.

items.title: 1,054 litterbugs nabbed by SWCorp since Jan 1
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Solid Waste and Public Cleansing Management Corporation (SWCorp) has caught 1,054 litterbugs nationwide since Jan 1. Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said yesterday all those caught would be prosecuted. Nga said most litterbugs were caught for not disposing of cigarette butts properly.

items.title: New British Airways route to boost KLIA connectivity
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: British Airways Plc will introduce a daily London to Melbourne flight via Kuala Lumpur starting Jan 9 next year, further cementing Kuala Lumpur International Airport's (KLIA) status as a strategic transit hub connecting Europe, Southeast Asia and Australia, Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) said. MAHB managing director Datuk Mohd Izani Ghani said the new route reflected strong confidence in KLIA as a leading Southeast Asian gateway. "The service enhances connectivity across three key regions of Europe, Southeast Asia and Australia, while strengthening Kuala Lumpur's role as an efficient transit hub for long-haul travel.

items.title: Scottish lawmakers vote down assisted dying bill
items.summary: LONDON: Scottish lawmakers rejected a bill on Tuesday that would have legalised assisted dying, as a similar bid in England and Wales stalls in Westminster's upper chamber. After debating for more than two hours, regional lawmakers voted down the bill by 69 votes to 57. Under the Assisted Dying for Terminally Ill Adults (Scotland) Bill, patients would have been eligible for an assisted death if they were "reasonably expected" to die within six months.

items.title: Eyeing trouble with RM10 contact lenses [WATCH]
items.summary: New Straits Times checks found at least 10 outlets selling contact lenses, including prescription lenses, raising concerns among eye specialists about potential health risk. Observations at the shops revealed that lower-powered lenses had mostly sold out, while higher-powered lenses remained available. Certain shops were also selling contact lens solutions.

items.title: 'Happened so fast': UK students panicked by meningitis outbreak
items.summary: HUNDREDS queued Wednesday to get vaccinated at the UK university campus at the heart of a deadly meningitis outbreak, as the number of cases rose to 20. By the weekend, one university student and one schoolgirl had died of the infection, with several others seriously ill in hospital in the outbreak, linked to a local nightclub. At least 200 students formed a long queue at the grassy campus near the medieval city of Canterbury, as the university rolled-out a targeted vaccination programme for meningitis B – a deadly bacterial strain.

items.title: Phantom flight: Iran war creates 9,100-km round trips to nowhere
items.summary: LONDON: As Emirates flight EK10 from London cruised over Saudi Arabia on Monday, news broke of a drone strike at its destination, Dubai. The aircraft turned back to Gatwick, flight data show, completing a 9,100 km (6,150 miles) round trip — one of dozens of "flights to nowhere" triggered by the Middle East war. Roughly 30 Emirates flights heading to Dubai International were also ordered back or rerouted after Iranian drone attacks temporarily shut what is normally the world's busiest airport for international passengers.

items.title: Nato allies in talks on 'best way' to re-open Hormuz strait
items.summary: OSLO: Nato chief Mark Rutte said Wednesday that the military alliance's members were discussing the "best way" to open the Strait of Hormuz, through which a large chunk of the world's oil supply normally passes. On Tuesday, Trump said "Nato is making a very foolish mistake" by not sending aid. "I've long said that I wonder whether or not Nato would ever be there for us.

items.title: Police probe viral altercation in Air Itam over firecrackers
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Police are investigating a viral altercation between two groups in Kampung Melayu, Air Itam, under Section 504 of the Penal Code for intentional insult with intent to provoke a breach of the peace. "The case has been classified under Section 504 of the Penal Code," he told the New Straits Times when contacted tonight. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: KJ says Zahid "gracious" during meeting on his Umno return
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin said Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi was "gracious" during their recent meeting. "Zahid was gracious and spoke extensively about what he has done in Umno and the party's plans for the next general election. Khairy also assured that his impending return to Umno does not mean the end of the podcast he co-hosts with former Umno Youth information chief Shahril Sufian Hamdan.

items.title: I'm innocent, France's Sarkozy tells appeal trial on Libya financing charges
items.summary: PARIS: Former French president Nicolas Sarkozy on Wednesday said he "did not commit any of the acts" he is accused of, as he took the stand at an appeal trial on charges he sought Libyan financing for his 2007 election. Sarkozy became modern France's first president to serve jail time last year after being convicted in an earlier trial on the same charges, spending 20 days behind bars. Sarkozy served 20 days before he was released pending the appeal.

items.title: Kuantan-bound express bus stopped at Penang Sentral after nail found in tyre
items.summary: Penang Road Transport Department (RTD) director Zulkifly Ismail said the tyre defect on the bus, which was scheduled to depart about 9pm, was discovered during an enforcement and advocacy operation conducted ahead of the Hari Raya Aidilfitri festive season. Zulkifly said the operation, which runs from March 13 to March 29, aims to ensure all buses are roadworthy before starting their journeys and to remind passengers to prioritise safety, including wearing seat belts. Drivers are generally following regulations," he said.

items.title: Malaysian Highway Authority steps up readiness as Raya traffic set to hit 3.5mil
items.summary: LLM director-general Datuk Ir Mohd Hadzmir Yusoff said the measures formed part of the agency's heightened readiness to manage the anticipated surge in traffic, projected to reach up to 3.5 million vehicles daily during the festive period. Hadzmir said traffic updates were continuously relayed to police patrol units for prompt action, while also being disseminated via LLM's social media platforms, including TikTok and Facebook. To support electric vehicle (EV) users, LLM is also expanding charging facilities along highways.

items.title: Cops nab 2 men over TM cable theft in Teluk Intan
items.summary: IPOH: Police have arrested two men suspected of involvement in the theft of Telekom Malaysia (TM) cables during an operation at Jalan Pasir Berdamar in Teluk Intan two days ago. Hilir Perak district police, in a statement on its Facebook page tonight, said interrogation of the suspects led to the seizure of a white sack containing coiled copper cables weighing an estimated 5.3kg. Police are continuing investigations to determine the suspects' involvement in other cable theft cases in the Teluk Intan district.

items.title: Pos Malaysia introduces fuel surcharge amid Middle East tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Pos Malaysia Bhd has introduced fuel surcharges on domestic and international shipments starting March 18 following the increase in fuel prices triggered by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The surcharge covers domestic services, such as Pos Laju Service, Pos Laju Prepaid (Blue), MyDistribution Services and Mel Plus Service. As for international shipments, the surcharge covers Redly Express, Redly Priority (EMS), Small Packet, Air Parcel, International Air Bulkmail and Flexipack International.

items.title: Pahang MB's Aidilfitri open house in Temerloh on March 22
items.summary: KUANTAN: People across Pahang are invited to attend the Aidilfitri open house hosted by Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Wan Rosdy Wan Ismail in Lanchang, Temerloh. Wan Rosdy said the open house, which serves as a platform to strengthen ties between state leaders and the public, would continue as usual this year. This marks the second occasion Wan Rosdy is hosting his Aidilfitri open house outside Kuantan.

items.title: Johor Baru grocery store fined RM5,000 for violating tobacco display regulations
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A grocery store was fined RM5,000 by the magistrate's court here today for displaying tobacco products in violation of regulations in October last year. According to the charge, the store was alleged to have displayed tobacco products in a manner that did not comply with requirements under Regulation 6(1) of the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health (Control of Sale) Regulations 2024. Johor Health Department prosecutor Mohd Fairuz Hamdan prosecuted.

items.title: Frederiksen hopes Danes will warm to her after fiery Greenland defence
items.summary: Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen has staked her leadership on defying United States President Donald Trump over Greenland. Frederiksen, whose Social Democrats suffered a historic defeat in municipal elections last November, has seen a revival this year which could make her Denmark's longest-serving leader since World War 2 if she completes another full term. Frederiksen, 48, entered parliament at 24 and has led the Social Democrats since 2015, becoming Denmark's youngest prime minister four years later and only the second woman to lead the Nordic country's government.

items.title: Middle East conflict: Evacuation suspended as airspace reopens
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The evacuation of Malaysians from the Middle East conflict zone has been suspended following the reopening of airspace in most countries in the region. "The government always prioritises the safety and welfare of Malaysians abroad. Under the Malaysian Evacuation Operation from the Middle East conflict zone, a total of 163 Malaysians returned home safely on March 11 via a special Malaysia Airlines flight.

items.title: Farage's Reform sees power withinits grasp
items.summary: THE old world is over. This is Nigel Farage's time. "The world is changing," Farage told Reuters as Reform starts fleshing out its policies, which include carrying out mass deportations, ditching international human rights treaties and slashing overseas aid.

items.title: Prolonged Middle East conflict could push oil prices even higher, analyst warns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Crude oil prices can go even higher than current levels of US$100-US$103 per barrel if the war in the Middle East continues affecting passageway in the Strait of Hormuz and supply tightness becomes more acute, Kpler senior crude oil analyst Muyu Xu said today. Crude oil prices are currently hovering at US$101-US$103 per barrel for Brent Crude while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) is around US$92-US$95 per barrel. However, Xu said that if the war ends by mid-April, prices are likely to see a quick and sharp downward correction to US$80-US$90 per barrel.

items.title: NST Leader: Extra holiday, a critique
items.summary: The government's decision to grant an additional public holiday for Hari Raya Aidilfitri — effectively creating an extended festive break — has been welcomed by many Malaysians, but criticised by the Malaysian Employers Federation (MEF) as an added financial burden. Hari Raya Aidilfitri is an important celebration for Muslims. Small wonder, workers welcome the extra holiday as it gives families that much more time to travel safely and reconnect with parents, siblings, extended family members and others in the kampung one grew up in.

items.title: Traffic builds up post-iftar, Rawang crash worsens gridlock
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Traffic on major highways nationwide worsened after iftar today, as vehicles surged towards hometowns ahead of the Aidilfitri celebrations. A spokesperson for the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) said severe congestion was reported at the Gombak Toll Plaza, with queues extending to the DUKE Highway near Batu Caves. "For northbound traffic, the stretch from Rawang Selatan to Bukit Beruntung is reported to be nearly at a standstill," the spokesperson told Bernama tonight.

items.title: PMO to rebuild five homes destroyed in Kuala Perlis fire
items.summary: KANGAR: The Prime Minister's Office (PMO) will assist in building five new houses for victims whose homes were destroyed in a fire at Kampung Pulau Ketam, Kuala Perlis, yesterday. Perlis Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Noor Amin Ahmad said the five houses destroyed in the fire will be rebuilt with an allocation of RM80,000 for each house. Noor Amin, who is also Perlis PKR State Leadership Council chairman, said state government approval regarding land is needed to enable construction to proceed.

items.title: Doesn’t matter what day is Raya, Kedah will holiday on Monday anyway
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, March 18 — The Kedah state government has declared Monday (March 23) an additional public holiday in conjunction with Hari Raya Aidilfitri, following the federal government’s announcement. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor said the state would observe the additional holiday regardless of whether 1 Syawal falls on Friday (March 20) or Saturday (March 21). Previously, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced an additional public holiday on Monday (March 23) if Aidilfitri falls on Friday (March 20), or on Friday (March 20) if the festival falls on Saturday (March 21).

items.title: US Navy vessels’ Penang stop was routine, approved by govt, says Khaled Nordin
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The recent presence of two United States Navy vessels in Penang was a routine port of call for logistical purposes that had received official approval from the Malaysian government. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin, in a statement, said that USS Santa Barbara (LCS 32) and USS Tulsa (LCS 16) docked at the North Butterworth Container Terminal (NBCT) from March 14 to 16. “Port of call visits by foreign naval vessels at Malaysian ports are not new and have long been part of established international defence relations.

items.title: Felda, Terengganu reach settlement on long‑running plantation land ownership issues
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) and the Terengganu state government today signed a settlement agreement to streamline the land ownership status of commercial plantations in six districts in the state. In a statement, Felda said the districts involved are Besut, Setiu, Dungun, Hulu Terengganu, Marang and Kemaman. Previously, the Terengganu state government issued notices prohibiting encroachment on land cultivated by Felda involving 10 plantations covering about 15,000 hectares.

items.title: Stay alert: MetMalaysia forecasts hot, dry Aidilfitri, public advised to stay hydrated, says minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Malaysians preparing to celebrate Aidilfitri are advised to remain vigilant over weather conditions, with dry and hot weather expected across most parts of the country from March 20 to 23. “For Saturday (March 21), rain is expected in Perak in the morning and will move to the Klang Valley and several areas in Sarawak by the afternoon,” he said in a statement today. Arthur added that on Sunday (March 22), weather conditions are expected to be generally fair nationwide in the morning and at night, except for light rain in parts of Sabah and Sarawak in the afternoon.

items.title: Storm, heavy rain warning for four states, KL, Putrajaya until 7pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in four states as well as the Federal Territories of Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya until 7 pm today. In a statement issued at 4 pm, MetMalaysia said the adverse weather is forecast to affect the whole of Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya, as well as several areas in Selangor - Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat and Sepang. The same warning also covers several areas in Perak - Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar, Bagan Datuk, Hilir Perak, Batang Padang and Muallim.

items.title: Johor folks go modest this Raya, buying essentials and recycling decor amid cloudy economy
items.summary: Bernama checks in Johor Bahru, Batu Pahat and Mersing found that people are avoiding unnecessary spending. Many are focusing on buying essential items and affordable clothing. In Mersing, contractor Nurfatimah Ibrahim, 37, said her family chose a simple approach by buying affordable items and making their own home decorations.

items.title: Car cloning in Malaysia: How syndicates turn stolen cars into ‘legitimate’ deals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — A recent police bust of a major car cloning syndicate has shed light on a sophisticated and deceptive vehicle fraud operating in Malaysia. What exactly is car cloning? At its core, car cloning is identity theft for vehicles.

items.title: Jobless man lands in dock for hurting wife’s new hubby with spanner in Dang Wangi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — An unemployed man pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of causing grievous bodily harm to a masseur using a dangerous weapon last week. Adam Muhammad, 37, was charged with causing grievous bodily harm to Sani, 30, by using a spanner at a premises in Dang Wangi here at 8.15 pm last March 10. Deputy Public Prosecutor Vivien Yeap Jie Xi did not offer bail because the victim suffered severe head injuries and received multiple stitches.

items.title: Customs uncover smoky business in Penang, bust three smuggling rings in BM, seize nearly RM4m in goods
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH, March 18 — The Royal Malaysian Customs Department (JKDM) has busted three syndicates smuggling cigarettes and liquor, seizing goods worth RM3.9 million in separate raids in Bukit Mertajam recently. Penang JKDM director Datuk Rohaizad Ali said four men were arrested in the raids at three different locations following intelligence gathered under Ops Petasan. “In the first raid at 1.40 pm on Feb 9, a team from the Enforcement Division of Penang JKDM raided and inspected a residential premises in Kampung Sungai Lembu and found 38,200 cigarettes and 1,238.15 litres of liquor.

items.title: Haj price still RM33,300: Tabung Haji locks in flight deals with Malaysia and Saudi Airlines, future might change if Gulf crisis persists
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The Pilgrims’ Fund Board (Tabung Haji–TH) has assured that the cost of performing the haj for the 1447H/2026 season will remain at RM33,300 despite global uncertainties. TH Group Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer Mustakim Mohamad said that although the ongoing conflict in West Asia could potentially impact costs, TH will absorb any unexpected increases. However, Mustakim said that TH may review future haj costs if the conflict in West Asia persists and significantly affects global oil prices.

items.title: For a safer Raya: Johor police to focus on 85 routes prone to crashes and jams statewide
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 18 — Johor police will monitor 47 hotspots (traffic congestion-prone areas) and 38 blackspots (accident-prone areas) throughout Ops Selamat 26 Hari Raya Aidilfitri, from tomorrow until Tuesday (March 24). Ab Rahaman said that Ops Selamat, themed “Kongsi Perjalanan, Kongsi Keselesaan, Keselamatan Diutamakan” (Share the Journey, Share Comfort, Safety First), focuses on three main objectives: preventing burglary, minimising road accidents and ensuring smooth traffic flow. Commenting on last year’s statistics, he said Ops Selamat recorded 1,642 accidents, with 26 fatalities, and issued 10,189 summonses for various traffic offences.

items.title: Alleged mastermind in Cikgu Chandra attack out on bail, police hunt for more suspects
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Police have released on bail seven suspects, including the alleged mastermind, linked to the recent attack on activist S. Chandrasegaran, but are still hunting for several others involved in the case. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar confirmed today that the seven individuals who were most directly involved have been released, while the search for the remaining suspects continues, The New Straits Times reported. “The seven suspects who were previously detained have been released on police bail,” Shazeli said.

items.title: Man sentenced to eight months’ imprisonment for damaging Sunway City temple
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — A man has been sentenced to eight months in prison after pleading guilty to desecrating a Chinese temple altar in Sunway City, Ipoh, last week. Perak police chief Datuk Noor Hisam Nordin confirmed the sentencing in a statement today. “A review of closed-circuit television (CCTV) footage found that an unidentified man had acted to damage the said temple on the previous night,” Noor Hisam said.

items.title: No queues, no chaos: Labuan ferry firms report smooth operations this Raya
items.summary: LABUAN, March 18 — The usually bustling atmosphere at Labuan’s two main ferry terminals ahead of Hari Raya Aidilfitri is noticeably absent this year, with both facilities reporting smooth operations and no signs of heavy congestion. Similarly, operations at the Labuan International Ferry Terminal appeared to be running as usual, with passenger boats maintaining regular schedules and no significant crowd of travellers seen at the terminal. Another operator, Labuan Mainland Link, is operating five daily trips using its ferries MV Goodwill Star, MV Putrajaya 1, MV Joystar and MV Kimanis 1 to cater to passenger demand.

items.title: ‘Hot spots’ or ‘black spots’, police zero in on Pahang’s 61 riskiest routes for festive travellers
items.summary: KUANTAN, March 18 — Pahang police have identified 36 accident-prone ‘black spots’ and 25 ‘hot spots’ statewide to be given priority attention during Ops Selamat, which runs from today until March 24. Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said the operation, held in conjunction with the Hari Raya Aidilfitri celebrations, aimed at ensuring the safety of road users and reducing road accidents, including fatalities, in the state. Yahaya said the operation would also involve cooperation with various agencies, including the Fire and Rescue Department, Civil Defence Force (APM) and Road Transport Department (JPJ).

items.title: Ports, factories, and illegal junk: Putrajaya gets serious on cleaning up Malaysia’s e-waste mess
items.summary: The Ministry of Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability (NRES) said the proposal was discussed at the NRES Special Enforcement Task Force Meeting No. In a statement today, NRES said the meeting emphasised stronger enforcement on environmental issues, including the entry of illegal e-waste. NRES said the government remains committed to strengthening enforcement against environmental offences, including illegal e-waste.

items.title: Friday or Monday? Sabah sets extra Raya public holiday based on Syawal date
items.summary: According to a State Government Gazette issued today, Governor Tun Musa Aman has declared Friday (March 20) as an additional public holiday if Aidilfitri falls on Saturday (March 21). The gazette also stated that if Aidilfitri is instead declared on Friday, the replacement public holiday will be observed on Monday (March 23). Last Sunday, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced a similar arrangement for additional Aidilfitri public holidays nationwide to facilitate travel and planning for the public.

items.title: DAP leaders publicly rebuke own deputy minister over appearance with activist Arun
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — Four DAP leaders today publicly rebuked Deputy Minister M. Kulasegaran for his presence at a court complex with an activist facing charges, saying the action invited “public controversy” and does not represent the party’s official stance. In a joint statement, DAP central executive committee (CEC) members Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali, Young Syefura Othman, and Syahredzan Johan, along with Bukit Bendera MP Syerleena Abdul Rashid, said they were in “complete disagreement” with their fellow party veteran’s actions. They said that Kulasegaran should have been more mindful of how his actions would be seen, given his official role as a deputy minister in the Prime Minister's Department.

items.title: The math is mathing: 2,000 chickens, RM7 per kilo, gone in 30 minutes at Bukit Gantang Raya sale
items.summary: BUKIT GANTANG, March 18 — About 2,000 chickens, priced at RM7 per kilogram, were snapped up within just half an hour today at the Jualan Termurah Madani programme held at the Bukit Gantang MP Service Centre in conjunction with Aidilfitri celebrations. Apart from chickens, eager shoppers also seized the chance to buy beef at as low as RM24 per kilogram, along with cooking oil, eggs, flour, sugar and other essential items in preparation for Aidilfitri. Meanwhile, Bukit Gantang MP Datuk Syed Abu Hussin Hafiz Syed Abdul Fasal said the bulk-purchase approach was implemented to provide lower prices to the public without the need for government subsidies.

items.title: Nilai urn theft: Suspect demands ransom via WhatsApp for 30 missing remains
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The case of 30 missing urns from a memorial park in Nilai has evolved into an extortion investigation after a suspect contacted the management demanding payment for the return of the cremated remains, police said. Nilai district police chief Supt Johari Yahya confirmed that the suspect reached out via WhatsApp using a foreign number, claiming to have found the urns. “The suspect then made a payment demand as a condition to return the remains,” Johari said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: Umbrellas up: MetMalaysia sounds storm warning for KL, Selangor, eight states
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a fresh weather alert for several regions across the country, with heavy rain, thunderstorms, and strong winds expected to persist until 5pm today. According to the advisory issued at 2.15pm, the affected areas in Peninsular Malaysia include the entire Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur as well as the Selangor districts of Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, and Petaling. In East Malaysia, the weather warning covers Sri Aman, Kapit (Song and Kapit), and Miri (Telang Usan) in Sarawak.

items.title: Indonesian man charged with causing teen’s death while carrying sickle in car
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — An Indonesian man was charged in the Teluk Intan Magistrate's Court today with two counts of dangerous driving, including one for causing the death of a teenager last week while allegedly carrying a long-handled sickle. The accused, Sutiyono, 39, who is hearing-impaired, nodded to show he understood the charges read before Magistrate Naidatul Athirah Azman, Berita Harian reported. For the first and more serious charge, Sutiyono is accused of causing the death of 18-year-old Adham Faisal through dangerous driving.

items.title: Tabung Haji posts highest profit in eight years, 3.5pc payout to benefit 9.7 million depositors
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The Pilgrims’ Fund Board (Tabung Haji -TH) today announced a profit distribution of 3.50 per cent, after zakat, for the 2025 financial year, the highest rate recorded since 2018. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Religious Affairs), Zulkifli Hasan, said the total profit distribution amounted to RM3.22 billion, benefiting more than 9.7 million TH depositors. Also present were TH Group Managing Director and Chief Executive Officer Mustakim Mohamad, TH Chairman Abdul Rashid Hussain, and TH Finance Department Group Executive Director Shamsul Bahar Shamsudin.

items.title: Penang police: Baby found unconscious at Seberang Jaya daycare died of asphyxiation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — A four-month-old baby boy who died while under the care of a daycare centre near Seberang Jaya Hospital last week was asphyxiated, police confirmed today based on initial autopsy findings. Penang Police Chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said investigation papers are nearly complete and will soon be referred to the Deputy Public Prosecutor for further action, Sinar Harian reported. The victim, identified as Hayl Haarith Mohd Syafaeez, was found unconscious at the 24-hour daycare, located within the hospital compound, in the early hours of last Monday.

items.title: Businesses need ready cash relief, wait-and-see approach could hurt MSMEs if Gulf crisis deepens, employers tells govt
items.summary: President Datuk Dr Syed Hussain Syed Husman, while acknowledging the government’s cautious approach, called for preparedness and flexibility in policy response should the situation in West Asia deteriorate. However, the government must remain ready to act swiftly if conditions worsen,” he said in a statement today. Syed Hussain said the situation reinforces the need for businesses to accelerate productivity enhancements, diversify supply chains, and adopt technology to mitigate future disruptions.

items.title: JKR projects see mixed progress, Kuching complex leads the way with one-third on track
items.summary: KUCHING, March 18 — About one-third of Public Works Department (JKR) projects nationwide are currently progressing ahead of schedule, said Deputy Works Minister Ahmad Maslan. He said that of the 855 ongoing projects, roughly one-third are either ahead of time or on track, while the remainder are behind schedule. Those that are behind schedule must be prioritised,” he said after inspecting the Kuching Federal Multipurpose Building Complex (BGP) construction site today.

items.title: Sarawak government aligns with Putrajaya, declares additional Hari Raya holiday
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — The Sarawak government has officially confirmed that it will observe the additional public holiday for Hari Raya Aidilfitri 2026, aligning with the recent announcement made by the federal government. The statement clarified that if Hari Raya falls on Friday, March 20, the additional holiday will be observed on the following Monday, March 23. However, if the first day of Syawal is on Saturday, March 21, then the preceding Friday, March 20, will be the public holiday.

items.title: Race against the clock: Indira Gandhi launches contempt action against IGP before missing daughter turns 18
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 18 — In a final and desperate bid to recover her long-abducted daughter, M. Indira Gandhi has initiated contempt of court proceedings against the Inspector-General of Police for the force’s decade-long failure to execute a court order to locate the child. The legal move comes with immense urgency, as Indira’s daughter, Prasana Diksa, is set to turn 18 early next month. Indira’s lawyer, Rajesh Nagarajan, said the action was taken due to the “lackadaisical attitude” of the police, Free Malaysia Today reported.
